summary: Consolidated two redundant chat loading indicators (a header banner and skeleton placeholders shown simultaneously) into a single centered spinner with message, matching the existing empty-state pattern for a cleaner, less jarring initialization experience.

The recording indicator in the meeting header used hardcoded dark colors that clashed with the app's theme and a static red dot that was easy to miss. Replaced with theme-aware destructive colors and an animated pulsing dot so the indicator adapts to light/dark mode and clearly signals that recording is active.

Redesigned the empty participants sidebar to lead with a clear message ("No one else has joined yet") and a prominent full-width copy-link button, replacing the previous cluttered layout that buried the call-to-action below a distracting status badge.

Repo health: 62/100 — Zero test coverage on critical real-time communication code is the biggest risk factor, compounded by loose TypeScript strictness settings that allow bugs to slip through undetected.

Improved the chat sidebar empty state microcopy from a vague "Start the conversation" to a clearer, action-oriented "Type a message below to chat with all participants" — giving users directional guidance on how to begin chatting.
Repo health: 58/100 — Zero test coverage across the entire codebase is the biggest risk, compounded by large monolithic files that make manual verification harder.

Fixed the metrics dashboard loading skeleton so card labels and icons stay stable while only placeholder values pulse. Previously the entire card flickered during load, making readable text jitter. Also widened the timestamp badge skeleton to match the actual badge width, reducing layout shift when data arrives.
Repo health: 72/100 — Zero test coverage and disabled TypeScript strict mode are the biggest factors holding back confidence in reliability.

Added descriptive aria-labels to the icon-only admit and reject buttons in the waiting room participant list. Screen readers now announce the action and participant name, improving accessibility for users who can't rely on visual icon cues alone.
Repo health: 62/100 — Exposed credentials in the environment file and zero test coverage are the biggest risks to production reliability and security.

Fixed inconsistent spacing in the participants sidebar empty state. Added horizontal padding to match participant list items and removed a conflicting width constraint on the "Copy meeting link" button, so the empty state no longer looks visually jarring compared to the populated list.

Participant video tiles now dim and gently pulse the avatar when a user is connecting or reconnecting. Previously the avatar appeared fully loaded, making the small spinner below it easy to miss — the tile now clearly communicates that the participant's connection is still in progress.
Repo health: 62/100 — Zero test coverage and a 2600-line WebRTC hook carrying too many responsibilities are the biggest factors holding back maintainability and confidence in changes.

When the chat connection fails, the message input area now shows a "Chat is temporarily unavailable" notice with a Reconnect button, instead of silently disappearing and leaving users confused about where the input went.

Added a toast notification when copying the meeting link in the participants sidebar, so users get clear confirmation the link was copied. Also extended the button feedback duration from 2 to 3.5 seconds to reduce the chance of missing it.
Repo health: 72/100 — Production debugging is severely hampered by global console suppression with no alternative logging service, making it nearly impossible to diagnose user-reported issues.

Replaced the hard-coded 85% height on the multi-participant video grid with a full-height flex layout, eliminating the blank strip at the bottom on tall mobile screens. Also added a "Waiting for others to join…" hint when the user is the only participant in grid view, matching the feedback already present in speaker view.

Fixed misleading microcopy in the participants sidebar empty state. The hint said "Share the link below" but no link was visible — only a copy button. Updated to "Copy and share the meeting link to invite others" so the instruction matches the actual UI.

Clarified vague device error messages in the pre-meeting setup screen. Instead of generic "check connection and allow access in browser settings," each device type now tells users exactly where to fix the issue — click the lock icon in the address bar for camera/mic, or check system sound settings for speakers.

Simplified the mobile meeting toolbar by removing triple-stacked shadows, redundant inner borders, and a decorative gradient divider. The two control rows now feel like one unified bar instead of two separate floating cards competing for attention.
Repo health: 58/100 — A 2,600-line monolithic hook and disabled TypeScript strict mode make this codebase fragile and hard to maintain safely at scale.

Toned down the participant reconnecting indicator from a warning-colored alert to a neutral muted style, matching the initial connecting state. Brief network hiccups now feel like a normal transient event instead of alarming the user with orange warning colors and assertive screen-reader announcements.

Fixed the Burnup chart widget's empty state which incorrectly showed a downward-trend icon, sending a confusing visual signal. Replaced it with an upward-trend icon to match the chart's purpose and updated the hint text to clearly explain that completed work trends upward.

The quick-add task form hid all submission and cancel hints on mobile, leaving users with no guidance. Added a mobile-friendly "Tap Create or tap outside to cancel" hint that replaces the desktop keyboard shortcut instructions on small screens.
